description: ZedMate Suite detects particle-like spots in 3D multi-channel microscopy stacks using a TrackMate Laplacian-of-Gaussian detector and quantifies per-channel intensities, with optional mimicry embedding of detected particles.
input_data: 3D multi-channel microscopy image stacks (anisotropic XYZ supported).
output_data: Spot/track detections with per-channel intensity measurements; optional mimicry-embedded particle image datasets.
use_when: Use this to detect spot-like particles in anisotropic 3D multi-channel microscopy data and quantify their intensities, optionally exporting mimicry-embedded particle datasets.
do_not_use_when: Do not use this when objects are not well approximated by spot-like particles or when you need full object segmentation rather than spot/track-based measurements.
typical_use_cases: Detecting virus-like particles in 3D fluorescence stacks and measuring per-channel intensities
Spot detection in anisotropic volumes by treating 3D as 2D detection plus tracking across Z
Creating mimicry-embedded particle datasets for neural network training (e.g., Virus-MNIST-like)

name: Zellige
description: Zellige is a Fiji plugin that automatically extracts a non-prescribed number of surfaces from a single-channel 3D fluorescence microscopy image stack and can generate projections and masks from the resulting height maps.
input_data: One-channel 3D fluorescence microscopy image stack opened in Fiji.
output_data: Surface height maps plus optional outputs including 2D projections (MIP), height-map projection image, extracted 3D surfaces, 3D surface mask, and an image sub-volume.
use_when: Use it when you need automated extraction of multiple surfaces from a 3D single-channel fluorescence stack and want height-map-based projections or masks for downstream analysis.
do_not_use_when: Do not use it for multi-channel inputs or non-3D images, since it requires a 1-channel 3D stack.
typical_use_cases: Extracting multiple epithelial surfaces from Drosophila pupal wing or abdomen 3D fluorescence stacks
Removing vesicle-like spurious signal by island removal before surface construction
Generating MIP-based 2D projections around each extracted surface using a deltaZ interval
Creating 3D surface masks to apply as ROIs on the original image

name: ZFBONE
description: ZFBONE is a Fiji/ImageJ macro toolset for rapid, reliable, semi-automated morphometric and intensity analysis of zebrafish bone structures (operculum, scales, caudal fin rays) for compound screening and phenotyping.
input_data: Standard image formats (TIFF, BMP), including 1- or 2-channel fluorescence images for operculum, RGB color images for scales, paired brightfield + corresponding fluorescence images for caudal fin, and microCT-derived images for ray intensity.
output_data: CSV result tables plus an 'analyzed' folder containing screenshots/annotated images, ROI Manager content (saved ROIs), and (for ray intensity) intensity plots and per-ray raw/aligned/normalized intensity profile data.
use_when: Use this for semi-automated quantification of zebrafish operculum, scale, caudal fin morphometry and mineralization/osteoclast readouts in osteoactive or osteotoxic compound studies.
do_not_use_when: Do not use this if you need a general-purpose or fully automated analysis pipeline outside the specific zebrafish bone assays supported by the provided macros.
typical_use_cases: Measure operculum mineralized area in larvae stained with alizarin red S or calcein
Quantify fluorescent bone cell content in transgenic larvae operculum images
Analyze scale area/shape and TRAP-positive or von Kossa demineralized area from RGB images
Assess regenerated caudal fin mineral content and ray morphometry from paired brightfield and fluorescence images
Extract and compare microCT caudal fin ray intensity profiles between control and treated groups

name: Zoom-in-movie
description: Zoom-in-movie is a simple tool for creating zoom-in movies with logarithmic approach and dynamic scalebar. Simply select ROI on your image and select Plugins/Zoom-in movie to run the plugin. Thanks to Eugene Katrukha for the initial implementation.
input_data: Fluorescence or biological microscopy images appropriate for this plugin's function
output_data: Processed images, measurements, or analysis results depending on the plugin used
use_when: You need Zoom-in-movie's specific functionality for your image analysis task
do_not_use_when: Standard Fiji tools or other registry plugins already cover your analysis needs
typical_use_cases: Applying Zoom-in-movie tools to fluorescence microscopy data
Integrating into multi-step image analysis workflows
